name: §2: Archetype Taxonomy
findings: Top-3 archetypes (domain_specific, regime_classification, ordering_hierarchy) account for 62% of all theorems

name: §3: Cross-Domain Structural Twins
description: Theorems with identical proof structures across different domains
findings: Largest twin families (same archetype + same test count, multiple domains):
[domain_specific] n_tests=4: 18 theorems across atomic_physics, continuity_theory, dynamic_semiotics, finance, materials_science
[domain_specific] n_tests=3: 14 theorems across astronomy, everyday_physics, kinematics, rcft, spacetime_memory
[domain_specific] n_tests=5: 10 theorems across atomic_physics, awareness_cognition, clinical_neuroscience, kinematics, quantum_mechanics
[ordering_hierarchy] n_tests=3: 9 theorems across astronomy, everyday_physics, evolution, materials_science, spacetime_memory
[ordering_hierarchy] n_tests=4: 9 theorems across continuity_theory, dynamic_semiotics, everyday_physics, evolution, materials_science
[ordering_hierarchy] n_tests=5: 8 theorems across awareness_cognition, clinical_neuroscience, consciousness_coherence, finance, quantum_mechanics
[regime_classification] n_tests=5: 8 theorems across atomic_physics, awareness_cognition, consciousness_coherence, gcd, materials_science
[domain_specific] n_tests=6: 7 theorems across awareness_cognition, everyday_physics, gcd, quantum_mechanics, unified_minimal_structure
[regime_classification] n_tests=3: 7 theorems across everyday_physics, evolution, rcft, spacetime_memory, weyl
[extremal_entity] n_tests=3: 6 theorems across astronomy, everyday_physics, evolution, rcft
geometric_slaughter: 6 theorems across 6 domains (clinical_neuroscience, continuity_theory, everyday_physics, evolution, gcd, quantum_mechanics...)
regime_classification: 13 theorems across 8 domains (astronomy, awareness_cognition, consciousness_coherence, evolution, materials_science, nuclear_physics...)
tier1_identity: 23 theorems across 15 domains (astronomy, clinical_neuroscience, consciousness_coherence, continuity_theory, dynamic_semiotics, everyday_physics...)

name: §4: First-Theorem Invariant
description: Does every domain bootstrap with identity verification?
findings: Domains where first theorem is NOT identity verification:
Identity-verification test counts: min=3, max=354, mean=52

name: §5: Geometric Slaughter Map
description: Where IC destruction from dead channels appears across domains
findings: Domains WITHOUT explicit slaughter theorems (12): astronomy, atomic_physics, awareness_cognition, dynamic_semiotics, kinematics, materials_science, nuclear_physics, rcft...

Structural insight: Geometric slaughter is the mechanism that connects IC ≤ F (integrity bound) to observable phenomena. A single dead channel (c_k → ε) collapses IC via exp(w_k·ln(ε)) while F barely moves.

name: §6: Regime Distribution Landscape
findings: gcd                            7 theorems: T-KS-6, T-KS-7, T-URC-2, T-URC-3, T-URC-5, T-URC-6, T-URC-7
nuclear_physics                4 theorems: T-NP-10, T-NP-2, T-NP-3, T-NP-4
Domains proving universal Collapse: astronomy, everyday_physics, evolution, materials_science

Structural pattern: Regime classification is the MOST COMMON archetype (43 theorems, 19.1%). This reflects 87.5% of the Fisher manifold being outside Stable — regime detection is the primary structural task.

name: §7: Falsification Forensics
findings: Name: T-F-10: Contagion as Correlated Channel Collapse
Statement: Collapsed entities share weakest channels from {debt, cashflow, regulatory}
Statement: Collapsed entities have Δ in top quartile and ω ≥ 0.20
Name: T-F-7: Geometric Slaughter in Finance
Statement: Entities with any channel < 0.10 have IC/F < 0.50
Statement: Safe haven assets have lowest Δ and highest IC/F ratio
Statement: Revenue growth and volatility control inversely correlated; high growth → high C, S
Falsified archetypes: regime_classification(2), geometric_slaughter(1), extremal_entity(1), correlation_structure(1)

DIAGNOSIS: All falsifications concentrated in 'finance'. This suggests the finance entity catalog has channel definitions that don't fully match the theorem expectations — a Tier-2 calibration issue, not a Tier-1 violation.

name: §8: Test Density Spectrum
findings: Test density: min=0, Q1=4, median=5, Q3=7, max=777
Dynamic range: 777× (from 0 to 777 tests per theorem)

name: §9: Identity Verification Network
description: Which Tier-1 identities are proven in which domains
findings: astronomy, clinical_neuroscience, continuity_theory, dynamic_semiotics, everyday_physics, evolution, finance, gcd, materials_science, nuclear_physics, quantum_mechanics, rcft, spacetime_memory, unified_minimal_structure
Domains with no explicit identity verification: atomic_physics, awareness_cognition, consciousness_coherence, kinematics, weyl

name: §10: Archetype Signatures per Domain
description: Domain fingerprints based on theorem archetype distribution
findings: Domain                        CO  CS  CC  XS  DS  EE  GS  ID  OH  PT  RC

name: §11: Emergent Structural Laws
description: Universal patterns that hold across all or most domains
findings: Every domain contains at least one identity-verification theorem
Exceptions: atomic_physics, awareness_cognition, kinematics, standard_model, weyl
Every domain contains at least one regime-classification theorem
Ordering/hierarchy theorems appear in ≥50% of domains
Domains with more entities produce exponentially more subtests
Theorems tend to either fully prove or clearly fail — few are close calls
Certain archetype pairs always appear together

laws.name: Identity Bootstrap Law
laws.statement: Every domain contains at least one identity-verification theorem

laws.name: Regime Ubiquity Law
laws.statement: Every domain contains at least one regime-classification theorem

laws.name: Hierarchy Prevalence Law
laws.statement: Ordering/hierarchy theorems appear in ≥50% of domains

laws.name: Entity-Density Scaling Law
laws.statement: Domains with more entities produce exponentially more subtests

laws.name: Binary Verdict Law
laws.statement: Theorems tend to either fully prove or clearly fail — few are close calls

laws.name: Archetype Co-occurrence Law
laws.statement: Certain archetype pairs always appear together
